programs.name: Medical Assistance/Medicaid
programs.what_it_does: Provides healthcare coverage for low-income seniors and disabled individuals, including long-term medical and personal care services. In Illinois, it is administered through the Department of Healthcare and Family Services for eligible older adults.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65+ and disabled individuals meeting income and asset limits.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers medical costs, long-term care, and waivers for home services.

programs.name: Home Services Program (HSP) Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services waiver under Medicaid to help seniors avoid nursing home placement. Provides personal care assistance, home-delivered meals, homemaker services, and transportation.
programs.who_its_for: Medicaid-eligible seniors aged 65+ who meet nursing facility level of care but prefer home-based care.
programs.benefit_value: Customized services based on assessed needs; prevents institutionalization.

programs.name: Illinois PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings, including adult day health care and home care. Integrates Medicaid and Medicare benefits for eligible participants.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified for nursing facility level of care but able to live safely in the community.
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive care; one-stop coordination of primary, acute, and long-term care services.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: Helps pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and cost-sharing through Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), and Qualifying Individual (QI) programs. Administered via Illinois Medicaid as assistance for low-income Medicare enrollees.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors on Medicare meeting specific income/asset thresholds (e.g., QMB up to 100% FPL).
programs.benefit_value: QMB: Full premiums/cost-sharing; SLMB: Part B premium; QI: Part B premium (up to $130+/mo savings).

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides funds via EBT card for low-income seniors to purchase food. Administered by Illinois Department of Human Services with senior-specific outreach.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 60+ (higher asset limits); households meeting income guidelines.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per person for eligible seniors.

programs.name: Illinois Home Weatherization Assistance Program (IHWAP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers energy assistance and home weatherization to reduce heating/cooling costs for low-income households. Includes insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs administered through local agencies.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors (priority for 60+) meeting income limits (e.g., ≤200% FPL).
programs.benefit_value: Free weatherization services; energy savings up to $1,500+ annually.

programs.name: Senior Health Insurance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free counseling on Medicare, Medicare Advantage, Part D, and supplemental insurance options. Helps Illinois seniors select and understand coverage to avoid errors and save money.
programs.who_its_for: All Medicare beneficiaries aged 65+, especially low-income and underserved seniors.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one assistance; potential savings of thousands on premiums/drugs.

programs.name: Community Care Program (CCP) Home Delivered Meals
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors through the Illinois Department on Aging's CCP. Provides at least one hot meal per day, 5 days/week, with nutritional support.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors aged 60+ assessed under CCP with functional or financial need.
programs.benefit_value: $7-10 per meal (often free/sliding scale); supports independence.

programs.name: Illinois Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides assistance including respite care, training, and counseling to family caregivers of older adults. Administered statewide by the Department on Aging to reduce caregiver strain.
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid family caregivers of adults aged 60+ or 55+ with Alzheimer's; income-based.
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $1,000/year; training and support services.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time paid community service work training for low-income seniors to gain skills and transition to unsubsidized employment. Administered through Illinois Department on Aging grantees.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income seniors aged 55+ (priority for hardest-to-serve).
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage pay (~$15/hr IL); work experience and job placement.

programs.name: Illinois Legal Aid Online / Senior Legal Helpline
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal assistance for common senior issues like benefits, housing, and consumer problems. Operates through statewide network including Legal Council for Health Justice.
programs.benefit_value: Free advice/representation; resolves issues like denied benefits.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, investigating complaints and ensuring quality care. Statewide program under Illinois Department on Aging protects rights.
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, assisted living, and other LTC facilities in Illinois.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy; resolves 80%+ of complaints without litigation.

programs.name: Illinois Senior Citizens and Persons with Disabilities Property Tax Relief
programs.what_it_does: Provides exemptions or freezes on property taxes for eligible seniors. Offers savings up to $750 yearly or tax deferral up to $7,500.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ with income under $65,000 who own and occupy their residence in counties like Cook.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific property tax exemptions and freezes beyond federal standards, varying by county like Cook County benefits.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $750 yearly savings or $7,500 tax deferral[1][5]

programs.name: Benefit Access Program (Seniors Ride Free & License Plate Discount)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free rides on CTA, Metra, Pace transit and reduces license plate sticker fees to $10. Streamlines application for multiple state transportation benefits.
programs.who_its_for: Illinois residents 65+ with household income up to $33,562 (1 person), $44,533 (2 people).
programs.whats_unique: Statewide consolidated program for free senior transit and vehicle discounts not universally available elsewhere.
programs.benefit_value: Free transit + $141 sticker savings ($10 vs $151); renews every 2 years[1][2]

programs.name: Health Benefits for Immigrant Seniors (HBIS)
programs.what_it_does: Provides Medicaid-like health coverage with no premiums or copays, including doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, dental, vision, and therapy. Covers seniors ineligible for federal Medicare/Medicaid due to immigration status.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Illinois seniors 65+ regardless of immigration status; enrollment paused as of 2025.
programs.whats_unique: First-in-nation state-funded health insurance for undocumented immigrant seniors, beyond federal programs.
programs.benefit_value: Full coverage (doctor, hospital, Rx, dental, vision); served 11,000+ enrollees[3]
